outlook.t1: S&P 500 hits all-time high 7,014 as Iran ceasefire talks reduce geopolitical risk premium
outlook.t2: VIX 17.48 — elevated but declining; defined-risk spreads remain the preferred structure
outlook.t3: Bull call spreads on SPY $710 target — leveraged upside with defined risk, IV crush tailwind
outlook.risk_note: Elevated volatility (VIX 17.48) favors defined-risk spreads over naked positions. Iran negotiations remain active.
outlook.expiry_note: Friday Apr 18 weekly expiry (3 DTE) — defined-risk bullish positions
outlook.trade_setup_note: Bull call spreads capture leveraged upside with defined risk; bull put spreads collect premium with a ~$4.50 buffer below the market. Both benefit from continued IV crush if geopolitical situation improves through Apr 18 expiry.
outlook.trade_disclaimer: Trade 1 (Bull Call Spread) and Trade 2 (Bull Put Spread) both expire Friday Apr 18 (3 DTE). The Hormuz blockade remains technically active — formal US-Iran talks have not yet been confirmed. Monitor Iran diplomacy headlines throughout the day. Confirm spread pricing before entering.
outlook.commentary_intro: The relief rally from Tuesday has put the S&P 500 firmly in all-time-high territory at ~7,014. SPY closed at $710.14 (+$8.36, +1.2%) Tuesday and the Nasdaq has now strung together 10 consecutive up days. Pre-market Friday is quiet as traders consolidate before the next Iran catalyst.
outlook.commentary_vix: VIX is holding at 17.48 (elevated zone, 15–20) — still elevated enough that premium-selling strategies benefit, but no longer in panic territory. IV has been draining steadily as Iran deal odds rise. WTI crude has tumbled ~7% on ceasefire expectations, a classic risk-on signal supporting the equity rally. The Dow has now turned positive for 2026.

active_trades.prob_profit: ~58–63% (ATM $695 call δ ≈ 0.50; need SPY > $700 by Fri)
active_trades.rationale: SPY closed at $710.14 (+$8.36, +1.2%) Tuesday — 10-day Nasdaq win streak, S&P at all-time high ~7,014. Bull call spread: buy ATM $695 call, sell $700 call (5-wide). Net debit ~$1.95; max profit $3.05 if SPY closes above $700 by Friday. VIX 17.48 (elevated) keeps ATM IV attractive. A confirmed Iran ceasefire announcement could gap SPY $5–10+ higher, making this spread highly profitable.

active_trades.leg_buy_put: SPY $685 put (long, further OTM — buffer protection)
active_trades.rationale: Bull put spread (credit): sell OTM $690 put (~4.50 points below $710.14), buy $685 put as protection. Collect ~$0.85 credit ($85/contract); max loss $415 if SPY drops below $685 by expiry. This is a premium-collection bullish trade that profits as long as SPY stays above $690 through Apr 18. With VIX 17.48 (elevated), the $690 put still carries meaningful premium. Any positive Iran news could send SPY sharply higher, making the $690 put deep OTM and this spread a winner at expiry.
